title: credential_stuffing
description: Customer accounts are being accessed by an attacker using credentials leaked in a third-party data breach, resulting in account takeovers and fraudulent transactions.

timeline.title: Anomalous Login Spike Detected
timeline.description: SIEM detects 5,000 login attempts over 2 hours with a 2% success rate, originating from a rotating pool of IP addresses.

timeline.title: Credential Stuffing Confirmed
timeline.description: Security team cross-references the attempted credentials against known breach databases and finds a match with a recently leaked dataset.

timeline.title: Customer Accounts Compromised
timeline.description: 100 customer accounts confirmed accessed by the attacker. Unauthorised session activity detected in multiple accounts.

timeline.title: Fraudulent Activity Detected
timeline.description: Several compromised accounts have been used to make unauthorised purchases and transfer funds.

timeline.title: Customer Notification Initiated
timeline.description: Affected customers notified by email. Compromised accounts locked pending password reset.

timeline.title: Bot Detection Deployed
timeline.description: MFA, device fingerprinting, and bot detection implemented on the login page to prevent future credential stuffing.

node-1.title: Unusual Login Spike Reported
node-1.situation: Customer support reports a surge in account takeover complaints. The SIEM shows 5,000 login attempts over 2 hours with a 2% success rate, using rotating IP addresses that suggest automated tooling. The credentials appear to be from an external source.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Investigate the login pattern and cross-reference the attempted credentials against known public breach databases.

options.label: B
options.text: Assume the customers have forgotten their passwords and direct them to the self-service reset portal.

options.label: C
options.text: Block the top 10 IP addresses and wait to see if activity continues.

node-2.title: Credentials Matched to Leaked Dataset
node-2.situation: Analysis confirms the credentials match a dataset leaked from a third-party e-commerce breach published last week. 100 customer accounts in your system share the same email/password combination. All are considered compromised.
node-2.question: How do you respond?

options.label: A
options.text: Lock all 100 compromised accounts immediately, notify affected customers, and force password resets before allowing access.

options.label: B
options.text: Only reset passwords for customers who call the support line to report suspicious activity.

node-3.title: Fraudulent Transactions Detected
node-3.situation: Review of the compromised accounts reveals several made unauthorised purchases and one initiated a fund transfer within 30 minutes of the attacker's login. The fraud team has been alerted.
node-3.question: How do you handle the fraudulent activity?

options.label: A
options.text: Immediately freeze all affected accounts, reverse unauthorised transactions where possible, and escalate confirmed fraud cases to the fraud response team.

options.label: B
options.text: Wait for customers to report the fraud individually before taking account action.

node-4.title: Preventing Future Credential Stuffing
node-4.situation: Customers have been notified. Fraudulent transactions are being reversed. Leadership asks how to prevent credential stuffing attacks from succeeding in the future.
node-4.question: What controls do you recommend?

options.label: A
options.text: Implement MFA for all customer accounts, add device fingerprinting, and deploy bot detection (e.g., CAPTCHA, behavioural analysis) on the login page.

options.label: B
options.text: Force a one-time global password reset for all customers and take no other action.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: All compromised accounts have been secured and fraudulent transactions reversed. MFA, device fingerprinting, and bot detection have been deployed on the login page. Affected customers have been notified and a lessons-learned review has been completed.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
